name: Castillo de Munera y Río Córcoles — circular in Munera
name_original: Castillo de Munera y Río Córcoles — circular en Munera
region_intro: Hiking around Mancha offers diverse landscapes within Castilla-La Mancha, central Spain, characterized by a significant number of protected natural areas. The region features two national parks, seven natural parks, and six river reserves, providing a variety of terrain for outdoor activities. Hikers can explore areas with interconnected lakes and waterfalls, unique wetlands, dramatic gorges carved by rivers, and mountainous regions with extensive forests and rock formations. Best hiking trails around Mancha The most popular hiking route is Ruidera High Lagoons Circuit, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the interconnected lakes and waterfalls of Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Senda del Ossero — circular (return via GR-114) through the Parque Natural de las Lagunas de Ruidera, a moderate 6.8 miles (11.0 km) path. This trail offers views of the natural park's aquatic ecosystem. Local hikers also love the Pie Enmedio Trail, a 4.3 miles (7.0 km) trail leading through natural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Hiking around Mancha is defined by natural parks with interconnected lakes, unique wetlands, and river-carved gorges. The extensive network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Mancha are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 690 hikers have used komoot to explore Mancha's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Mancha?
A: Mancha offers a diverse range of landscapes, from the interconnected lakes and waterfalls of the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park to the unique wetlands of Tablas de Daimiel National Park. You'll also find dramatic gorges carved by rivers like the Tagus and Gallo in Alto Tajo Natural Park, mountainous regions such as the Sierra de Cuenca, and areas with fascinating rock formations like Ciudad Encantada.

Q: Are there many hiking trails available in Mancha?
A: Yes, Mancha features a comprehensive network of trails. There are over 50 hiking routes available, catering to various skill levels. This includes nearly 30 easy routes, over 20 moderate trails, and several more challenging options.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Mancha?
A: Absolutely. Many of Mancha's natural parks offer easy, accessible paths suitable for families. For example, the Tablas de Daimiel National Park features easy hikes on wooden walkways, perfect for nature observation. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373884762/pie-enmedio-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pie Enmedio Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 7 km route that takes about 1 hour 50 minutes to complete. The Las Caras Trail near Buendía also offers an open-air museum experience with sculptures, ideal for families.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Mancha?
A: Yes, Mancha has numerous circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373874047/ruidera-high-lagoons-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruidera High Lagoons Circuit&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.4 km trail that explores the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park. Another excellent circular option in the same park is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373833700/senda-del-ossero-circular-return-via-gr-114-through-the-parque-natural-de-las-lagunas-de-ruidera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Ossero — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11 km long.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls in Mancha?
A: Yes, the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park is particularly known for its system of interconnected lakes and waterfalls. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanas Lagoon Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or explore the general area of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4659461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Ruidera&lt;/a&gt; to experience these natural features. The Los Calares del Río Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park also features the spectacular 80-meter waterfall at the source of the Mundo river.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking in Mancha?
A: Mancha is rich in historical sites. You can hike through landscapes dotted with iconic windmills, particularly in Consuegra and Campo de Criptana, which evoke the tales of Don Quijote. Many routes also pass near medieval castles and fortresses, such as Castillo de Belmonte or Castillo de Almansa. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077279971/cueva-de-montesinos-ermita-de-san-pedro-castillo-de-rochafrida-molino-del-tobar-circular-lagunas-de-ruidera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Montesinos, Ermita de San Pedro, Castillo de Rochafrida, Molino del Tobar — circular&lt;/a&gt; route in Lagunas de Ruidera includes several historical points of interest.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mancha?
A: The trails in Mancha are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 120 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse natural beauty, from the tranquil lagoons to the dramatic river gorges, and appreciate the variety of routes available for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, Mancha offers several difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064807357/guadiana-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 19 km and presents a more demanding experience through the region's natural landscapes.

Q: Can I explore unique geological formations on hikes in Mancha?
A: Certainly. Mancha is home to several unique geological formations. The Ciudad Encantada (Enchanted City) in Cuenca features fascinating rock formations that can be explored via hiking routes. Near Toledo, the Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña offer impressive reddish clay formations sculpted by nature. The Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park also showcases caves, sinkholes, and waterfalls.

Q: Are there any art-themed trails in Mancha?
A: Yes, Mancha offers unique trails that combine nature with art. The Las Caras Trail around the Buendía reservoir features approximately 20 sculptures carved into stone, creating an open-air museum. Another option is the Ruta del Barranco del Río Madera in Albacete, a 6 km route adorned with over 60 stone and wood sculptures along the gorge.

Q: What are some notable natural parks to visit for hiking in Mancha?
A: Mancha is rich in protected areas. Key parks for hiking include Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park with its interconnected lakes, Tablas de Daimiel National Park known for its wetlands, and Alto Tajo Natural Park, which features extensive forests and gorges. Other notable parks are Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park and Los Calares del Río Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park.
tips: It is located on a hill overlooking the valley of the river Júcar. It was built in the 13th century in the Mudejar style and had a keep that is still preserved today. In the 1960s, restoration work began to preserve the building and restore its historical value.

Known as the "Roman Bridge", although its construction is believed to date back to the medieval period, around the 14th century. It is made of stone, with three semicircular arches and a total length of approximately 30 meters.
The mural is the work of the artist Emilio Solana, and is dedicated to the Wedding of Camacho. The Camacho Wedding Days are also organized annually in the area.
An important source of water for the locality of Munera, as it traverses its municipal term and irrigates its fields and gardens.

waypoints.name: Mural of the Wedding of Camacho
